title: Hate Leads to Lollipops

"'''Hate Leads to Lollipops'''" was a lighthearted story by [[David McCaig|Dave McCaig]] in ''[[Star Wars Tales 9]]'' about a young [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]. It was collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 3]],'' [[Star Wars: Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir (trade paperback)|''Star Wars: Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir'' trade paperback]] and ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Rise of the Sith Vol. 1]]''.

The art and plot are reminiscent of children's cartoons or [[Wikipedia:Mad magazine|''Mad'' magazine's]] ''Spy vs. Spy'' comic strips, and there is minimal dialogue.

==Plot summary==
[[File:MaulsDreamsOfLollipops-HLTL.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Maul escapes from the orphanage.]]

During [[Darth Maul/Legends|Maul's]] early years, he was sent to the [[Happy Nerf Herder]], a maximum security home for wayward boys on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. While there, he was involved in a breakout and, upon escaping, went on a hunt for a lollipop. After using a cable to get to the nearby [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Fish Market, Maul stole a [[Skimboard/Legends|hoverboard]] from a bystander, accidentally causing an explosion by doing so. He used this hoverboard to get to [[RIB hutt]], where he crashed into an unknown [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] before stealing a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] from a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who was at the restaurant.

Maul used this lightsaber to jump off the side of the building and slow his landing by slicing the lightsaber down the building's side. Though the Jedi chased him, Maul eventually landed upon a [[Granite slug/Legends|granite slug]] which had attached itself to the building and dropped the lightsaber before landing on an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] along with the slug. The airspeeder went along, out of control, before catapulting both Maul and the slug onto a nearby building which contained a lollipop shop. Before Maul was able to get his sought-after lollipop, however, he was taken captive by a [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guard]], who brought him before [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Though the Guardsman wanted to take him back to the security home, Palpatine stated that Maul simply needed better guidance and that he would re-educate the boy personally. Maul finally got his lollipop as Palpatine placed one in his mouth.
